title: grants — domain events
description: Twelve events. Lifecycle events (Issued / Updated / Expired / Revoked) carry the AccessGrant model fresh from the DB. Security-critical events (SelfServiceGrantAttempted / GrantCrossTenantAttempt / GrantDenialIssued / GrantDenialRevoked) fire on audit-worthy conditions and always route to compliance. Cache-observability events (GrantEvaluationCacheHit / GrantEvaluationCacheMiss) sampled to keep volume manageable. All lifecycle events implement ShouldDispatchAfterCommit so listeners never fire against a rolled-back transaction; security events fire IMMEDIATELY (pre-commit) so a rolled-back attempt still leaves an audit trail.

events.name: GrantIssued
events.trigger: GrantIssuer::issue() completes: row inserted, permissions validated, tenant + grantor guards passed. First domain event on the happy path.
consumers: grants::InvalidateGrantEvaluationCache (drop L1 + L2 cache entries for the subject)

events.name: GrantUpdated
events.trigger: PATCH /grants/{id} completed — only `expires_at` + `reason` are mutable (never subject/resource/permissions/decision after issue). Fires only when a real change happened.

events.name: GrantExpired
events.trigger: PurgeExpiredGrantsJob observed `expires_at <= now()` on a non-terminal grant. Transitions the row to revoked with revoked_reason='expired'. Terminal — no listener can reverse.
consumers: notifications::DispatchGrantExpiredNotification (to subject; to grantor if opt-in setting enabled)

events.name: GrantRevoked
events.trigger: Explicit revocation via DELETE /grants/{id} or grantor action. Terminal.

payload.revoker: User|null (null for system revokes — reconcile orphaned)

events.name: GrantDenialIssued
events.trigger: A grant with decision='deny' was created. FIRES IMMEDIATELY (not afterCommit) so audit captures every attempt even if the outer transaction rolls back.
consumers: audit::WriteHighSeverityAuditRow (severity=critical — denies are inviolable, every one is notable)

events.name: GrantDenialRevoked
events.trigger: A deny grant was revoked (lifting an inviolable block). Also audit-critical — someone re-enabled access that was explicitly denied.

events.name: BulkGrantIssued
events.trigger: BulkGrantIssueJob finished processing a CSV batch. Fires ONCE per batch (not per row) so downstream analytics can aggregate correctly.

events.name: SelfServiceGrantAttempted
events.trigger: A grantor tried to grant PERMISSIONS THEY DO NOT HOLD (privilege escalation attempt). Refused by observer with GRANT_PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION (403). FIRES IMMEDIATELY (pre-refusal) so audit captures every attempt.

events.name: GrantCrossTenantAttempt
events.trigger: Someone tried to issue a grant where subject.tenant_id !== resource.tenant_id !== grantor.tenant_id — a tenant-isolation breach attempt. Refused by observer with GRANT_CROSS_TENANT (422). FIRES IMMEDIATELY (pre-refusal).
consumers: notifications::DispatchTenantIsolationBreachNotification (to platform ops — this is a platform-level security signal)

events.name: GrantResourceOrphaned
events.trigger: ReconcileOrphanedGrantsJob discovered a grant whose resource_id no longer resolves (resource hard-deleted out-of-band). The reconciler auto-revokes the grant with revoked_reason='orphaned_resource' before firing this event.

events.name: GrantEvaluationCacheHit
events.trigger: GrantOverlayResolver::resolve() returned a decision from the L1 or L2 cache. Sampled per `grants.telemetry.cache_hit_sample_ratio` (default 0.01) to keep volume manageable.

events.name: GrantEvaluationCacheMiss
events.trigger: GrantOverlayResolver::resolve() missed both caches and hit the DB. Never sampled — every miss is interesting because they should be rare after warm-up.

notes: The security-critical events (SelfServiceGrantAttempted / GrantCrossTenantAttempt / GrantDenialIssued / GrantDenialRevoked) fire SYNCHRONOUSLY, not afterCommit. Rationale: even a rolled-back transaction must leave an audit trail for these — the ATTEMPT is what needs recording, not the outcome. Every one of these routes to a critical-severity audit row.
Cache observability events (GrantEvaluationCacheHit / Miss) are the RED-pattern instrumentation for the resolver's hot path. Miss events feed the SLO — target < 5% miss rate after cold-start.
GrantIssued / GrantUpdated / GrantRevoked lifecycle events dispatch afterCommit so downstream ACL cache invalidation only fires for durably-persisted rows.
